metadata.description: Comprehensive B2B prospect intelligence database for Georgian cheese distribution. Each prospect represents a brand/company with one or more physical locations. All locations verified via Google Places API with exact GPS coordinates.
metadata.coverageZone: 10-hour drive radius from Columbus, OH (~600 mile radius)

GEO_REST.rationale: Georgian restaurants use 200-600 lbs/mo for khachapuri, sulguni dishes, cheese platters. Higher end for multi-dish menus with adjaruli, imeruli, megruli khachapuri types.

GEO_BAKERY.rationale: Bakeries producing 50-200 khachapuri/day use 400-1200 lbs/mo. Highest volume per-location category. Shredded cheese packs are the primary product.

GEO_MARKET.rationale: Retail sales of cheese wheels, shredded packs, nadughi, butter for direct consumer sale. Volume depends on store foot traffic and local Georgian community size.

CAUC_REST.rationale: Caucasian restaurants with Georgian items on menu use less than pure Georgian places. Typically 2-5 cheese-based dishes. Good conversion potential.

RUS_REST.rationale: Russian restaurants may carry 1-3 Georgian dishes. Khachapuri is a popular cross-cultural item. Steady but lower volume per location.

UZB_REST.rationale: Uzbek restaurants serve khachapuri as cross-cultural menu item. Limited but consistent cheese usage.

ARM_REST.rationale: Armenian cuisine uses similar fresh white cheeses. Potential for sulguni as string cheese/chechil alternative.

EE_MARKET.rationale: Eastern European groceries sell cheese to diaspora community. Volume depends on store size and proximity to Georgian/Russian community.

CA_MARKET.rationale: Central Asian markets serve communities familiar with sulguni-type cheeses. Moderate retail volume.

DISTRIBUTOR.rationale: Distributors aggregate demand across 50-500+ accounts. Single highest-volume deal type. Lower per-lb price (wholesale) but massive cumulative volume.

ARTISAN_PIZZA.rationale: Artisan pizzerias may use sulguni for 1-3 specialty pizzas. Small volume but premium pricing for unique product.

INTL_MARKET.rationale: International gourmet stores carry specialty cheeses at premium markup. Moderate volume, premium positioning.

CHEESE_SHOP.rationale: Artisan cheese shops buy small quantities at highest per-lb prices. Story-driven sales — "American-made Georgian cheese" is a compelling narrative.

MEDITERRANEAN_REST.rationale: Mediterranean restaurants could use sulguni as halloumi alternative (grills identically, similar salt level). Requires sales education.

BRUNCH_UPSCALE.rationale: Upscale brunch spots may feature cheese boats (adjaruli) or nadughi as premium ricotta alternative. Low volume but high-margin channel.

CATERING.rationale: Catering companies order event-driven. Inconsistent frequency but potentially large per-event quantities. Cheese boats are visually stunning at events.

MEAL_KIT.rationale: Meal kit companies buy in bulk at lower margins but potentially huge volume. "Make Your Own Khachapuri" kit could be a viral product.

CRITICAL.description: Highest priority — immediate outreach. Georgian restaurants, bakeries, markets, and high-volume distributors.

MEDIUM.description: Good fit — requires targeted sales pitch. Russian restaurants, Central Asian markets, some pizza makers.

LOW.description: Possible fit — lower conversion probability. Distant locations, smaller volume categories.

EXPLORATORY.description: Experimental — requires market education. Cheese shops, brunch spots, catering, meal kits.
